name: The Dalles Bioterror
description: The Dalles bioterror attack, also known as the Rajneeshee bioterror attack, was a deliberate poisoning of salad bars in The Dalles, Oregon, in 1984. Followers of Bhagwan Shree Rajneesh, the spiritual leader of the Rajneesh movement, sought to influence the outcome of a local election by incapacitating voters. They contaminated salad bars with Salmonella typhimurium, resulting in 751 people becoming ill. The attack is considered the first and, to date, the largest bioterrorist attack in United States history. Proponents of broader conspiracy theories sometimes link the event to government agencies or other groups, suggesting a larger agenda beyond influencing local elections. Mainstream perspectives, based on extensive investigations and legal proceedings, attribute the attack solely to the Rajneesh movement and its leaders' attempts to manipulate the election. Skeptics point to the overwhelming evidence presented in court, including confessions from those involved, and the lack of credible evidence supporting alternative theories. Academic research focuses on the sociological and psychological factors that contributed to the event, the failures of public health systems, and the legal and ethical implications of bioterrorism.
subtitle: Rajneeshee Salmonella poisoning of The Dalles.

claims.claim: Rajneeshees deliberately poisoned salad bars to influence an election.
claims.evidence: Confessions from Rajneeshpuram residents, physical evidence of Salmonella contamination, and the timing of the attack before the election.
claims.criticism: Mainstream consensus accepts this as the primary motive. Some fringe theories suggest this was a smokescreen for a larger, unknown purpose.

claims.claim: The attack was orchestrated by high-ranking members of the Rajneesh movement.
claims.evidence: Evidence presented at trial, including testimony and documents implicating Ma Anand Sheela and other leaders.
claims.criticism: Mainstream accounts focus on Sheela and her inner circle. Some argue Bhagwan Shree Rajneesh was aware of or involved in the plot, though direct evidence is lacking.

claims.claim: The government or other entities were involved in the attack or its cover-up.
claims.evidence: Isolated claims and circumstantial evidence cited by conspiracy theorists, such as perceived inconsistencies in the official investigation.
claims.criticism: No credible evidence supports this. The investigation was thorough, and the perpetrators were brought to justice. Claims of government involvement are largely speculative.

keyFigures.name: Ma Anand Sheela
keyFigures.role: Secretary to Bhagwan Shree Rajneesh and key orchestrator of the attack.

keyFigures.name: Bhagwan Shree Rajneesh
keyFigures.role: Spiritual leader of the Rajneesh movement.

keyFigures.name: Lynn Weaver
keyFigures.role: Oregon state epidemiologist who investigated the outbreak.

timeline.event: The Dalles salad bar poisoning occurs.
Ma Anand Sheela and other Rajneeshpuram leaders flee the community.
Sheela and others are extradited and convicted of various crimes, including assault and poisoning.

sources.author: Carter, Lewis F.

sources.title: Carter, Lewis F.
